title: OSMM Optimization Recommendation Object
description: Learning-category object: a prioritized, actionable change proposed from the learning loop (Phase 7 sub-process 7.6), derived from Customer Insights and Performance Measurements. Where a Customer Insight is the diagnosis, an Optimization Recommendation is the prescription. It writes forward into the Work Product / Strategy layer via lean pointer-plus-prose targets, and carries its own disposition (proposed -> accepted/rejected/implemented) distinct from the object lifecycle status. Canonical schema for object_type 'optimization_recommendation'. The osmm-optimization-recommendation-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

optimization_recommendation_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'OPR-' + lowercase hyphen slug describing the change (e.g. 'OPR-wendys-checkout-fee-transparency'). Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

status.description: Object lifecycle state (the standard's draft/proposed/stable/deprecated). Distinct from 'disposition', which tracks whether the recommendation was acted on.

name.description: Short readable label for the recommendation (e.g. "Add fee transparency to app checkout").

recommendation.description: The proposed change itself — the prescription, stated as an action (e.g. "Surface all fees on the checkout review screen and A/B test against the current flow").

rationale.description: Why this change — the reasoning that connects the evidence to the proposed action.

derived_from.description: The evidence chain behind the recommendation. Optional.

linked_customer_insights.description: Customer Insights this recommendation acts on.

linked_performance_measurements.description: Performance Measurements that motivate the recommendation directly.

priority.description: Relative priority for acting on the recommendation. Optional.

expected_impact.description: What improvement is expected if implemented — directional or quantified. Optional.

disposition.description: Whether the recommendation has been acted on. Distinct from the lifecycle 'status'. Optional; defaults to proposed in practice.

targets.description: The write-forward: the Work Product / Strategy objects this recommendation would change (e.g. Marketing Strategy MKS-, Campaign Strategy CMS-, Offer OFR-, Creative Strategy CRS-, Journey JNY-, Experience EXP-). A lean pointer plus prose — applying a change produces a new version of the target; this object records the proposal. Optional.

target_id.description: Id of the object the change targets.

recommended_change.description: Prose description of the change to that object.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context. Use 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date.
